steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content: The left side, ending with “cinema,” is a complete independent clause with subject Nanette Kelley and verb joined. The right side, beginning with “Her film,” is also a complete independent clause with subject Her film and verb is about.

steps.title: Apply the independent clause separation rule
steps.content:
Two independent clauses require a period, semicolon, or comma plus a coordinating conjunction for proper separation.

Choice B uses a period, correctly separating the two independent clauses.

The other choices violate this: A uses only a comma (comma splice), C's “and” doesn't properly connect them, and D lacks any punctuation (run-on).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
